title: Protocol Treasury
summary: A lesson on treasury thinking as a long-horizon resource allocation problem rather than a passive pool of capital.

bodySections.title: Treasury as Strategy
bodySections.body:
A treasury is not only a balance. It is a mechanism for deciding how resources support the network's future priorities.

That includes maintenance, ecosystem growth, research, and the resilience needed to keep development aligned with protocol goals.

bodySections.title: Allocation and Accountability
bodySections.body:
Once capital is viewed as strategic infrastructure, allocation decisions need to be legible and accountable.

This lesson frames treasury management as part of how a protocol turns long-term intent into durable execution.

quizQuestions.prompt: Why is the treasury described as more than just a balance?
What does treasury allocation help translate into action?
